text:
Market today: Risk-on mood today. Iran deal removes a near-term tail risk, oil stabilizing above $70 takes pressure off inflation fears, and China factory data beating on tech export demand is directly supportive of your chip/semi names. Monday rallies after weekend geopolitical resolutions have a real track record per the headlines - momentum is your friend this morning.

Your holdings: Three need a look. SOXL (-7.06%) is your biggest concern - it's a 3x leveraged semi ETF that decays daily, and a -7% loss on a leveraged product on margin means your real pain is amplified hard; respect that $200 stop. SMH (-0.69%) is mild but watch it as a leading indicator for your whole book. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - tiny move against you gets ugly fast on margin.

Watch: Whether SMH and SOXL catch the China tech export tailwind today. Also watch SOXL's $200 stop - if it cracks, cut without hesitation.

Bottom line: Let winners run, but keep one eye locked on SOXL - that's where your real risk lives today.

CRWD (3.26%, already a winner): Scanner flags an ACCUMULATE 8/12 at entry 742.17-741.42, stop 740.31. You already own it with a 3.26% gain. The setup is all three timeframes pointing up (Grade A bias)
